# Deep Dive: Startup Legitimacy Theatre

> **Research boundary:** This material is for analysis, ethics, detection, and academic writing. It is not an operational guide for deceptive growth or platform manipulation.

## Core idea

Startups compete for belief before they have durable proof. That makes metrics and associations powerful legitimacy signals.

## Legitimacy signals

- ARR.
- Growth rate.
- Waitlist size.
- Customer logos.
- Security/compliance claims.
- Accelerator affiliation.
- Investor names.
- Press coverage.
- Launch badges.
- GitHub stars.

## Manipulation modes

| Mode | Description | Risk |
|---|---|---|
| Metric inflation | Number is exaggerated or undefined | Investors/customers overestimate traction |
| Metric ambiguity | ARR, revenue, GMV, run-rate, contracts blurred | Apples-to-oranges comparison |
| Screenshot theatre | Dashboard images shown without context | Unverifiable proof |
| Logo laundering | Weak/expired customer relationship implied as major validation | Borrowed authority |
| Compliance theatre | Trust claims overstate assurance | Enterprise risk distortion |
| Press loop | Coverage of claimed traction becomes evidence of traction | Narrative feedback loop |

## Audit questions

- Define the metric precisely.
- Ask whether it is audited, recurring, collected, contracted, or projected.
- Separate gross volume from net revenue.
- Ask for retention/cohort context.
- Check whether logos represent paying, active, permissioned customers.
- Identify company denial or unresolved allegations in contested cases.
